titleStmt.title: The Triumph of Mark the Mourner
The Paradise

sourceDesc.p: Ernest A. Wallis Budge, The Book of Paradise, Being the Histories and Sayings of the Monks and Ascetics of the Egyptian Desert by Palladius, Hieronymus and Others: The Syriac Texts, According to the Recension of ʿAnân-Îshôʿ of Bêth ʿÂbhê, Edited with an English Translation, vol. 1, Lady Meux Manuscript 6 (London: W. Drugulin, 1904), p. 197.

Transcription of the text downloaded from Wikisource on 10/27/2024: https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/The_Paradise/Volume_1/Book_2/Chapter_1 . This Wikisource transcription is available under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike License

body.head: Chapter 1. The Triumph of the Blessed Man Mark the Mourner
body.ab: Macarius the priest told us the following story:—At the time when I was administering the Holy Offering I took good heed unto Mark the mourner, and I never gave it to him, but an angel did so from the altar; I saw, however, the palm of the hand of the angel who gave it unto him. Now this Mark was a young man, and he could repeat by heart the New and the Old Testaments; he was meek beyond measure, and both in body and in thought he was purer than many.
